events: ISM Manufacturing PMI June 2026 released at 10:00am ET today (53.3 — expansion)
June Jobs Report (nonfarm payrolls) released Thu Jul 2 at 8:30am ET (markets closed Fri Jul 4)
US equity markets closed Fri Jul 3 in observance of Independence Day (Jul 4 falls on Saturday)
Next FOMC meeting: Jul 28-29, decision Jul 29 at 2:00pm ET
Q2 2026 earnings season begins in earnest mid-July
rates: steady — Fed held at 3.50%–3.75% at Jun 17 meeting; next decision Jul 29; ~80% market odds of no change
valuation: elevated — trailing P/E ~25, CAPE ~39.9 (125% above historical average); forward P/E ~20
q2_market_performance: S&P 500 +14% in Q2 2026, best quarter since 2020; Nasdaq +20% in Q2
futures_this_morning: S&P 500 futures -0.23%, Nasdaq futures -0.81% — modest pullback after strong quarter
ism_manufacturing: 53.3 in June — 20th consecutive month of expansion; new orders 56.0
tariff_risk: Section 122 universal 10% tariff authority expires Jul 24; administration signaling further tariffs (copper 50%, Brazil 50%) in coming weeks
note: Market is technically in an uptrend (SPY 7.8% above 200d average) and finished Q2 with exceptional gains. However, valuation is stretched (trailing P/E ~25, CAPE ~40), inflation remains above the Fed's 2% target, and tariff policy is actively unsettled with a key authority expiring July 24. Futures are mildly negative this morning. The June jobs report drops tomorrow (Thu Jul 2) — a strong print could reignite rate-hike fears. We hold 100% cash and have no positions. In this environment, we demand a genuine margin of safety before buying, not just a fair price.
